title: Valerianella samolifolia
taxonomy: Valerianella samolifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae, commonly known as the valerian family. This family is part of the order Dipsacales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the honeysuckle and the hawthorn. Valerianella samolifolia is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant is also known as samphire or glasswort, and is often found growing in coastal areas and on rocks. It is a small, annual herb that produces small white flowers in the summer months.
uses: Valerianella samolifolia is known to have various medicinal uses. It has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and constipation.
